summary: Residents may understand who should inherit the home informally, but formal systems require legal documentation.
intervention_meaning: Clear public guidance and trusted navigators can reduce confusion before families enter formal legal processes.
quote: People don't know what they don't know.

summary: Unclear heirs, disagreement, or unreachable family members can delay title clearing and repair decisions.
intervention_meaning: Interventions may need to support family decision-making before the issue becomes a legal bottleneck.
quote: If they can't come to consensus, then in many ways the property may sit in limbo.

summary: Legal support and title-clearing services can be difficult to access or afford, especially when residents are on fixed incomes.
intervention_meaning: Low-cost deed clinics and service navigation can target barriers before they lead to property loss.
quote: So rather than finding it out in court, which is too expensive; they just let it go. And so that is, I think, I would say that's probably the biggest.

summary: Residents may hesitate to engage legal or government systems because of distrust, fear, or prior negative experiences.
intervention_meaning: Community-based messengers may make formal systems more usable and less intimidating.
quote: Instead of waiting for people to come to you, go to them.

summary: Tangled title risk interacts with property value, disinvestment, redlining, and racial wealth gaps.
intervention_meaning: Spatial targeting should avoid causal overclaiming while identifying where outreach and legal support may be most needed.
quote: Most of the times it is the Black Butterfly region.
